channel.title: ProbBrain - AI News Terminal
channel.description: AI lab announcements, arXiv papers, GitHub and HuggingFace trending, AI Hacker News, and independent writers - aggregated, deduped, and posted to @ProbBrain on Telegram.

item.title: [Latent Space] The Next War Is Already Here. The West Isn't Ready. — Yaroslav Azhnyuk, The Fourth Law & Guest Host Noah Smith, Noahpinion
item.description: The Next War Is Already Here. The West Isn't Ready. — Yaroslav Azhnyuk, The Fourth Law & Guest Host Noah Smith, Noahpinion

item.title: [GitHub Trending] Imbad0202/academic-research-skills — Academic Research Skills for Claude Code: research → write → review → revise → finalize
item.description: Academic research skills framework for Claude Code provides tools for research, writing, review, revision, and finalization workflows.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] HKUDS/CLI-Anything — "CLI-Anything: Making ALL Software Agent-Native" -- CLI-Hub: https://clianything.cc/
item.description: GitHub trending project CLI-Anything enables software to become agent-native.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] ggml-org/llama.cpp — LLM inference in C/C++
item.description: llama.cpp provides C/C++ based inference for large language models.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] tech-leads-club/agent-skills — The secure, validated skill registry for professional AI coding agents. Extend Antigravity, Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot and more with absolute confidence.
item.description: GitHub project agent-skills provides a validated skill registry for integrating extensions into AI coding agents like Claude Code, Cursor, and Copilot.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] BigBodyCobain/Shadowbroker — Open-source intelligence for the global theater. Track everything from the corporate/private jets of the wealthy, and spy satellites, to seismic events in one unified interface. Hook an AI agent up to have it parse through data and find previously unseen correlations. The knowledge is available to all but rarely aggregated in the open, until now.
item.description: Open-source intelligence platform Shadowbroker aggregates data on private jets, satellites, and seismic events in a unified interface with AI agent support.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] humanlayer/12-factor-agents — What are the principles we can use to build LLM-powered software that is actually good enough to put in the hands of production customers?
item.description: Repository presents 12-factor principles for developing production-grade LLM-powered software.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] NVlabs/Sana — SANA: Efficient High-Resolution Image Synthesis with Linear Diffusion Transformer
item.description: SANA uses a linear diffusion transformer for efficient high-resolution image synthesis.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] microsoft/ai-agents-for-beginners — 12 Lessons to Get Started Building AI Agents
item.description: Microsoft's ai-agents-for-beginners provides 12 lessons for learning to build AI agents.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] ZhuLinsen/daily_stock_analysis — LLM驱动的 A/H/美股智能分析：多数据源行情 + 实时新闻 + LLM决策仪表盘 + 多渠道推送，零成本定时运行，纯白嫖. LLM-powered stock analysis system for A/H/US markets.
item.description: Open-source LLM system analyzes A-share, Hong Kong, and US stock markets using real-time data and news to generate trading signals.

item.title: [Hacker News] 'We mould trees to grow into the shape of chairs'
item.description: 'We mould trees to grow into the shape of chairs'

item.title: [Hacker News] Enough with the AI FOMO, go slow-mo, says Domo CDO
item.description: Enough with the AI FOMO, go slow-mo, says Domo CDO

item.title: [Hacker News] AI eats the world (Spring 26) [pdf]
item.description: AI eats the world (Spring 26) [pdf]

item.title: [Hacker News] Linux security mailing list 'almost unmanageable'
item.description: Linux security mailing list 'almost unmanageable'

item.title: [Hacker News] Eric Schmidt speech about AI booed during graduation
item.description: Eric Schmidt speech about AI booed during graduation

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] AgentStop: Terminating Local AI Agents Early to Save Energy in Consumer Devices
item.description: AgentStop predicts and terminates unpromising LLM-agent trajectories early, reducing energy consumption by 15-20% with minimal performance loss on consumer devices.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] TeamTR: Trust-Region Fine-Tuning for Multi-Agent LLM Coordination
item.description: TeamTR framework improves multi-agent LLM coordination by using trust-region fine-tuning to address sequential fine-tuning's compounding occupancy shift.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Quantization Undoes Alignment: Bias Emergence in Compressed LLMs Across Models and Precision Levels
item.description: Quantizing language models to lower bit widths causes new stereotypical biases that standard evaluation metrics fail to detect.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Mask-Morph Graph U-Net: A Generalisable Mesh-Based Surrogate for Crashworthiness Field Prediction under Large Geometric Variation
item.description: Mask-Morph Graph U-Net uses barycentric morphing and node masking to improve crash simulation surrogate generalization to varied mesh geometries.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] MuteBench: Modality Unavailability Tolerance Evaluation for Incomplete Multimodal Fusion
item.description: MuteBench benchmark evaluates 6 fusion architectures across 9 clinical datasets for robustness to missing multimodal data, finding architecture family is the strongest predictor of robustness.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Reducing the Safety Tax in LLM Safety Alignment with On-Policy Self-Distillation
item.description: On-policy self-distillation reduces the safety-reasoning tradeoff in LLM safety alignment better than off-policy methods.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Logical Grammar Induction via Graph Kolmogorov Complexity: A Neuro-Symbolic Framework for Self-Healing Clinical Data Integrity
item.description: Logic-GNN detects data corruption versus legitimate clinical extremes in healthcare using temporal graph neural networks and Kolmogorov complexity.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Reading the Cell, Designing the Cure: Perturbation-Conditioned Molecular Diffusion for Function-Oriented Drug Design
item.description: Researchers propose CURE, a molecular diffusion model that designs drugs by conditioning on desired gene expression patterns.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Privacy Evaluation of Generative Models for Trajectory Generation
item.description: Generative trajectory models (GANs, VAEs, diffusion models) are vulnerable to membership inference attacks; privacy is not inherently protected.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] GQLA: Group-Query Latent Attention for Hardware-Adaptive Large Language Model Decoding
item.description: GQLA extends latent attention with hardware-adaptive decoding paths, allowing single trained weights to efficiently run on both H100 and commodity GPUs.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] PDRNN: Modular Data-driven Pedestrian Dead Reckoning on Loosely Coupled Radio- and Inertial-Signalstreams
item.description: PDRNN is a modular RNN-based system for pedestrian positioning that fuses multimodal sensor data with uncertainty estimates.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Position: Ideas Should be the Center of Machine Learning Research
item.description: Paper proposes Ideas First framework emphasizing behavioral signatures over benchmark-driven engineering and disconnected theory in ML research.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Curriculum Learning of Physics-Informed Neural Networks based on Spatial Correlation
item.description: Curriculum learning framework for Physics-Informed Neural Networks addresses spatial coupling to improve training stability and reduce optimization failures.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Training on Documents About Monitoring Leads to CoT Obfuscation
item.description: Models trained on documents describing CoT monitoring systems learn to obfuscate reasoning and evade detection.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Tadpole: Autoencoders as Foundation Models for 3D PDEs with Online Learning
item.description: Tadpole, an autoencoder foundation model for 3D PDEs, pre-trains on synthetic data and transfers to dynamics learning and generative modeling tasks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Universal Approximation of Nonlinear Operators and Their Derivatives
item.description: First universal approximation theorems proven for nonlinear operators and their derivatives in Banach spaces via operator learning architectures.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] GQA-{\mu}P: The maximal parameterization update for grouped query attention
item.description: Maximal update parameterization scalings for grouped-query attention enable hyperparameter transfer across different model architecture configurations.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] GESD: Beyond Outcome-Oriented Fairness
item.description: GESD is a procedural fairness metric measuring disparities in explanation stability and robustness across protected subgroups.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] How Data Augmentation Shapes Neural Representations
item.description: Researchers demonstrate how data augmentation strength reshapes neural representation geometry and predicts ensemble improvement.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Time-Varying Deep State Space Models for Sequences with Switching Dynamics
item.description: Time-varying state-space neural networks using basis functions outperform time-invariant models on switching dynamics and speech denoising tasks with comparable complexity.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Beyond Bounded Variance: Variance-Reduced Normalized Methods for Nonconvex Optimization under Blum-Gladyshev Noise
item.description: Variance-reduced normalized methods achieve O(ε^-4) minimax-optimal convergence rates for nonconvex optimization under quadratic-growth noise.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] From Weight Perturbation to Feature Attribution for Explaining Fully Connected Neural Networks
item.description: Weight perturbation method for FCNN feature attribution addresses occlusion technique limitations while achieving competitive performance with existing approaches.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] From I/O to Code with Discovery Agent
item.description: DIO-Agent synthesizes programs from input-output examples using evolutionary search guided by LLM mutations and a bias toward simpler hypotheses.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Bounded-Rationality, Hedging, and Generalization
item.description: Researchers model generalization as a bounded-rational hedging problem between fitting training data and controlling sample influence using information-geometric tradeoffs.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] LEAP: Trajectory-Level Evaluation of LLMs in Iterative Scientific Design
item.description: LEAPBench evaluates LLMs in iterative scientific design using trajectory metrics, finding they underperform classical Bayesian optimization baselines.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] PACER: Acyclic Causal Discovery from Large-Scale Interventional Data
item.description: PACER guarantees acyclicity by construction for causal discovery, scaling efficiently to high-dimensional data with two orders of magnitude speedup.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Controllable Molecular Generative Foundation Models
item.description: CoMole combines motif-aware graph diffusion with reinforcement learning to enable controllable molecular generation with improved validity and performance across benchmarks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Federated Learning of Spiking Neural Networks under Heterogeneous Temporal Resolutions
item.description: Federated learning framework addresses temporal resolution mismatch in spiking neural networks across heterogeneous edge devices.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] PRB-RUPFormer: A Recursive Unified Probabilistic Transformer for Residual PRB Forecasting
item.description: Probabilistic Transformer model forecasts cellular network spectrum availability with uncertainty quantification, enabling shared learning across multiple carriers and sectors.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Is One Score Enough? Rethinking the Evaluation of Sequentially Evolving LLM Memory
item.description: SeqMem-Eval measures LLM memory quality across multiple dimensions, showing aggregate metrics miss critical failure modes like forgetting and negative transfer.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Always Learning, Always Mixing: Efficient and Simple Data Mixing All The Time
item.description: OP-Mix unifies data mixing across LLM training phases using low-rank adapters, improving pretraining perplexity 6.3% with reduced compute.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Fluency and Faithfulness in Human and Machine Literary Translation
item.description: Research across 130k literary translations reveals tradeoff between translation fluency and semantic faithfulness in human and machine outputs.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] DiscoExplorer: An Open Interface for the Study of Multilingual Discourse Relations
item.description: An open-source web interface called DiscoExplorer provides access to multilingual discourse relation datasets spanning 16 languages.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Automatic Construction of a Legal Citation Graph from 100 Million Ukrainian Court Decisions: Large-Scale Extraction, Topological Analysis, and Ontology-Driven Clustering
item.description: Researchers extracted 502 million citation links from 100 million Ukrainian court decisions to construct a legal citation graph revealing domain boundaries and predicting legislative importance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Greedy or not, here I come: Language production under vocabulary constraints in humans and resource-rational models
item.description: Study shows humans use greedy algorithms for language production under vocabulary constraints, with skilled speakers showing more revision behavior.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Adesua: Development and Feasibility Study of an AI WhatsApp Bot for Science Learning in West Africa
item.description: WhatsApp-based AI science tutor Adesua completed feasibility study in Ghana with 56 users, achieving 93.75% helpfulness rating.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Eskwai for Students: Generative AI Assistant for Legal Education in Ghana
item.description: Eskwai, a retrieval-augmented generation system, served 3.1K Ghanaian law students with 32K queries over 2.5 years while raising ethical education concerns.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Capability Conditioned Scaffolding for Professional Human LLM Collaboration
item.description: Framework conditions LLM assistance on user expertise level to prevent over-reliance on AI in unfamiliar domains.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Neural Activation Patterns Across Language Model Architectures: A Comprehensive Analysis of Cognitive Task Performance
item.description: Analysis of six LLM architectures reveals mathematical reasoning produces highest attention entropy and decoder models exhibit greater sparsity than encoder models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Why are language models less surprised than humans? Testing the Parse Multiplicity Mismatch Hypothesis
item.description: Study tests whether language models' simultaneous consideration of multiple sentence parses explains their lower surprisal than humans; finds parse count alone doesn't.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Reasoning Models Don't Just Think Longer, They Move Differently
item.description: Reasoning-trained models show altered hidden-state trajectories on harder problems, beyond increased length, with strongest effects in code tasks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Retrieval-Augmented Large Language Models for Schema-Constrained Clinical Information Extraction
item.description: Retrieval-augmented generation pipeline extracts structured clinical data from nurse-patient transcripts with 80.36% F1 accuracy.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] FINESSE-Bench: A Hierarchical Benchmark Suite for Financial Domain Knowledge and Technical Analysis in Large Language Models
item.description: FINESSE-Bench introduces a suite of eight benchmarks with 3,993 questions to hierarchically evaluate financial knowledge and reasoning capabilities in large language models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] RoPE Distinguishes Neither Positions Nor Tokens in Long Contexts, Provably
item.description: RoPE positional embeddings provably fail to distinguish positions and tokens in long contexts, with effectiveness approaching random chance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] DetectRL-X: Towards Reliable Multilingual and Real-World LLM-Generated Text Detection
item.description: DetectRL-X introduces a multilingual benchmark to evaluate LLM-generated text detectors across eight languages and six domains with real-world scenarios and attacks.
